data.description:
## Summary
The designed un-archive seam — rebar revert <id> <archived-event-uuid> — is broken end to end: ticket-revert.sh removes the .archived marker and _is_net_archived (ARCHIVED minus REVERT) is implemented for the list fast-path, but the reducer's process_revert only appends a revert record; replay still applies the ARCHIVED event, so compiled state stays status=archived/archived=True and the ticket remains hidden.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified in sandbox against installed 0.4.0 == HEAD engine, temp store; discovered while validating the fix for bug bored-grain-wok / f803)
1. rebar archive <id>; note the ARCHIVED event uuid (event file in .tickets-tracker/<id>/).
2. rebar revert <id> <archived-uuid> -> exit 0; .archived marker removed.
3. rebar list -> [] (ticket still hidden); rebar show <id> -> status: archived.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: reverting the ARCHIVED event restores the pre-archive state (visible in list, status open).
- Actual: revert is recorded but the archived projection survives replay.

## Source
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket_reducer/_processors.py process_revert (~:255) — appends to reverts, never touches state["archived"]/state["status"].
- Working halves: ticket-revert.sh:180-196 (marker removal), ticket_reducer/_api.py _is_net_archived (+ :281 fast path).

## Fix direction
In process_revert, after the append:
if data.get("target_event_type") == "ARCHIVED" and state.get("archived"): state["archived"] = False; and if status is exactly "archived", restore "open" (preserves deleted semantics — process_archived keeps deleted status). Validated in sandbox. CAVEAT: .cache.json has no reducer-version key, so previously-cached reverted tickets stay hidden until cache invalidation — bump/add a cache version with this change (relates to bug fond-ulna-ball).

## Suggested RED test
Reducer-level: ARCHIVED then REVERT(target=ARCHIVED) replay -> archived=False, status=open, ticket present in reduce_all_tickets default projection. RED today.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] revert of an ARCHIVED event makes the ticket visible in default list with status open (RED test first)
- [ ] deleted tickets are NOT resurrected by reverting an ARCHIVED event
- [ ] Cache invalidation handled (version key or documented self-heal)
- [ ] test_ticket_reducer_marker.py and archive/revert suites green
data.title: revert of an ARCHIVED event never un-archives: process_revert appends a record but leaves the archived projection intact
